contexts.name: Server: Docs + Sweeps + OpenAPI
contexts.description: Document publishing/editing endpoints, background sweep operations, and OpenAPI spec generation, with pg integration tests.

contexts.name: Server: MCP Agent Surface
contexts.description: MCP stdio agent surface (memory_search/context/add + entity_lookup), authenticated as the developer's personal token so RLS applies per tool call.

contexts.name: Server: REST HTTP + Auth + Entrypoint
contexts.description: REST memory API on :8600 + bearer-token->principal auth + server entrypoint/wiring and graceful shutdown.

contexts.name: Server: Governance Console API
contexts.description: Governance console REST API: approve/reject promotions, resolve contradictions, entity graph + governance-health analytics powering the Next.js console.

contexts.name: Governance Console API
contexts.description: Maintainers approve/reject knowledge promotions, resolve contradictions, and read the entity graph and governance-health analytics that power the Next.js console.

contexts.name: MCP Agent Surface
contexts.description: Coding agents connect over MCP stdio (memory_search, memory_context, memory_add, entity_lookup) authenticated as the developer's personal token so RLS applies to every tool call.

contexts.name: REST Memory API & Auth
contexts.description: Agents and users add memories and run permission-aware hybrid search over the org knowledge base via REST on :8600; every request resolves a bearer token to a principal before touching data.

contexts.name: Server: Projects + Onboarding Pairing
contexts.description: Project registry + repo whitelist (the unit API keys scope to) and the device-flow onboarding pairing: unauthenticated start/poll, console-approved claim that mints a project-scoped read+write key exactly once, and the served brainiac-onboard skill.

contexts.name: Server: Library Distribution API
contexts.description: Library distribution surface (LIBRARY-PLAN LB1): REST /v1/library/* (lib:read reads + usage, lib:publish adopt/deprecate/ratify) and the MCP tools standards_for / skill_search / skill_fetch / skill_report_usage; integration-tested over real HTTP and JSON-RPC.

contexts.name: Gateway: BYOM Model + Resilience + Health/Redact
contexts.description: BYOM model gateway (Qwen/DashScope + deterministic mock), resilience (retry/circuit-breaker), health, and secret redaction.

contexts.name: Pipeline: Divergence + Reembed + Integration Tests
contexts.description: Divergence detection + embedding reembed (model-change backfill) plus the pipeline integration tests.

contexts.name: Pipeline: Worker + Compose + Ingest
contexts.description: Background worker draining the ingest queue + document composition (the anti-rot compose loop) writing memories with provenance under governance, plus the sampled runtime citation-faithfulness judge on needs_review revisions.

contexts.name: Pipeline: Extract + Resolve + Contradict + Policy
contexts.description: Knowledge extraction pipeline core: extract candidates from transcripts (and harvested OKF bundle documents), resolve entities, detect contradictions, apply promotion policy.

contexts.name: BYOM Model Gateway
contexts.description: Bring-your-own-model provider trait with Qwen/DashScope (OpenAI-compatible) and a deterministic mock provider; records per-call token usage so orgs pay with their own keys.

contexts.name: Pipeline Worker & Ingest Queue
contexts.description: Background worker that drains the ingest queue: picks up enqueued sources, runs the extraction pipeline, and writes memories with provenance under governance rules.

contexts.name: Knowledge Extraction & Resolution
contexts.description: Turns raw LLM session transcripts into structured memories: extracts candidate knowledge, resolves entities, detects contradictions with existing memories, and applies promotion policy.

contexts.name: Pipeline: Library Mining Sweep
contexts.description: LB3 passive mining (LIBRARY-PLAN): deterministic sweeps proposing standard candidates from unclaimed drifts, reinforced practices, and settled contradictions; dedup honors rejections for a window (rejection is knowledge). Sweep kind `library` on the schedule table.

contexts.name: Pipeline: Standards Pages (L8)
contexts.description: LIBRARY-PLAN L8: the org's adopted rules rendered as a per-stack KB page. Rides the whole document layer (dirty-marking, revisions, review gate, health breaker, Confluence) but is PROJECTED deterministically — no LLM: a ratified sentence must never be re-worded by a model.

contexts.name: Core: Fusion + Temporal + Metrics + Scoring + Rerank
contexts.description: Core retrieval math: score fusion, temporal as-of logic, quality metrics, scoring, and reranking.
contexts.businessFeature: Core: Fusion + Temporal + Metrics + Scoring + Rerank

contexts.name: Core: Domain Types + Embeddings
contexts.description: Core domain vocabulary (memories, principals, statuses, visibility) + the embedding abstraction used by every crate.

contexts.name: Store: Documents + Feedback + Publishing + Tokens
contexts.description: Newer store modules: documents (published pages), feedback, publishing state, and API tokens.

contexts.name: Store: Orgs + Entities + Governance
contexts.description: Store repositories for orgs/teams/members, canonical entities + merges, and governance records under the caller's RLS transaction.

contexts.name: Store: Memories + Hybrid Retrieval + Queue
contexts.description: Memory persistence + hybrid (vector+lexical) permission-aware retrieval with pgvector, plus ingest-queue store ops.

contexts.name: Core Domain Types & Embeddings
contexts.description: Shared domain vocabulary (memories, principals, statuses), embedding abstraction, retrieval score fusion, temporal as-of logic, and quality metrics used by every crate.

contexts.name: Database Schema & Migrations
contexts.description: Postgres 16 + pgvector schema with row-level security policies, migration files, and the docker-compose stack that provisions the database.

contexts.name: Org, Entity & Governance Store
contexts.description: Repository layer for orgs/teams/members, canonical entities with merges, and governance records (promotions, contradictions) — all executed under the caller's RLS transaction.

contexts.name: Memory Store & Hybrid Retrieval
contexts.description: Persists memories and serves hybrid (vector + lexical) permission-aware retrieval with pgvector, plus the ingest queue store operations.

contexts.name: Store: Library (Standards + Skills + Usage)
contexts.description: Library-layer data plane (LIBRARY-PLAN LB0, migration 0028): standards as rule-atoms with versions and provenance, the divergence-ratification bridge, versioned skill bundles (drafts never served), and team-attributed usage events; org-scoped RLS.

contexts.name: Eval: Docs/Contradiction/Extraction/Resolution/Pipeline Profiles
contexts.description: Eval profiles: docs, contradiction, extraction, resolution, and pipeline quality profiles + their pg tests.

contexts.name: Eval: Harness Core + Retrieval/Grid Profiles
contexts.description: Eval harness core: seed the synthetic org, run retrieval/grid profiles, apply quality gates, emit comparable JSON reports.

contexts.name: Fixtures: Golden Loader + Validate + Schema
contexts.description: Golden fixture loader: load/validate/assign deterministic IDs to the versioned Meridian corpus.

contexts.name: Architecture Docs & Workspace Config
contexts.description: Product and system documentation (architecture, eval methodology, plan, embeddings research) plus the Cargo workspace manifest — the source of truth for how Brainiac is built and measured.

contexts.name: Meridian Fixture Corpus
contexts.description: The synthetic 'Meridian' organization golden dataset: org/team layout, gold memories, entities and merges, contradiction cases, temporal as-of cases, retrieval Q&A + permission-leak probes, and raw session transcripts.

contexts.name: Golden Fixture Loader
contexts.description: Loads, validates, and assigns deterministic IDs to the versioned Meridian fixture corpus so evals and tests run against a known-good synthetic org.

contexts.name: Meridian Eval Harness
contexts.description: CI quality gate: seeds the synthetic Meridian org, runs retrieval-quality profiles against gold Q&A and leak cases, and emits comparable JSON reports.

contexts.name: Console: Health + Analytics + Observatory + Ops
contexts.description: Health + Analytics + Observatory + Divergence + Ops (sweep control) dashboards.

contexts.name: Console: Docs Reader + Editor
contexts.description: Docs Reader + Editor: read/edit published knowledge documents via server actions; markdown rendering.

contexts.name: Console: Memory Archive + Inspector
contexts.description: Memory Archive + Inspector: browse memories and inspect one memory's detail/provenance.

contexts.name: Console: API Keys Management
contexts.description: API Keys Management: create/list/preview/revoke the org's API keys (project-scoped keys show their project), plus the copyable brainiac-onboard skill bootstrap.

contexts.name: Console: Projects & Onboarding Desk
contexts.description: Projects module: the project registry + repo whitelist (the onboarding allow-list) and the pairing approval rail — approve derives the project from the whitelist match; unmatched requests can only be denied.

contexts.name: Console: Ingest Monitor
contexts.description: Ingest Monitor: submit sources into the ingest queue and watch the live processing feed.

contexts.name: Console: Knowledge Graph Explorer
contexts.description: Knowledge Graph Explorer: entity graph (CortexMap + StarChart/DepthOfField views) with canonical hubs + evidence.

contexts.name: Console: Disputes Bench
contexts.description: Disputes Bench: maintainers triage reader FEEDBACK claims on a single memory (reverified/deprecated/dismissed) via server actions. NOTE: this is NOT contradiction adjudication — the contradiction endpoints (POST /v1/reviews/contradictions/{id}/resolve) are server-complete but have no console UI.

contexts.name: Console: Promotion Review Queue
contexts.description: Promotion Review Queue: maintainers approve/reject pending promotions via server actions (the governance gate).

contexts.name: Console: Audit Ledger
contexts.description: Audit Ledger: reverse-chronological governance feed (promotion reviews, contradiction resolutions, dispute resolutions) from GET /v1/audit, with kind filtering and server-side paging on the real `total`. States plainly that the console gate is a shared org token, not per-user identity — actor_id names which token decided, never which person.

contexts.name: Analytics Observatory & Knowledge Graph
contexts.description: RETIRED 2026-07-15: superseded by the granular Console: * contexts after the module refactor. Kept as a tombstone; owns no files.

contexts.name: Promotion Review Queue
contexts.description: RETIRED 2026-07-15: superseded by the granular Console: * contexts after the module refactor. Kept as a tombstone; owns no files.

contexts.name: Console Shell & Home
contexts.description: The governance console's app shell, landing/home experience, offline-API fallback, and the shared design theme and demo data that give the console its look.

contexts.name: Console: Library Modules (Standards + Skills)
contexts.description: Library console station (LIBRARY-PLAN LB2): standards board (stack/category/rule tree, rule detail with provenance + per-team pulse + versions, triage gate with decree flow) and skills catalog (pulse-ranked cards, drafts marked unservable); live/demo via withDemoFallback; also wired into the /demo tour and home station 06.

contexts.name: Console: Demo Mode + Login
contexts.description: Demo mode (public, no-backend) + login (passcode/session auth).

contexts.name: Console: Next.js API Routes
contexts.description: Next.js BFF API route handlers: status, keys, ingest, memory-by-id, graph-canonical proxying to :8600.

contexts.name: Console: API Client + Types + Auth
contexts.description: Typed API client to the Brainiac REST server (:8600), shared response types, auth helpers, demo-fallback.

contexts.name: Console: Home + Station Modules
contexts.description: Console home/landing experience and the six-station module grid.

contexts.name: Console: Shell + Nav + Design System
contexts.description: Console app shell + navigation + design system (root/console layouts, chrome, theme, routes config, shared components).

contexts.name: Console Build & Test Config
contexts.description: Next.js, TypeScript, PostCSS, and Vitest configuration plus package manifest that build and test the console.

contexts.name: Brainiac API Client & Types
contexts.description: Typed client the console uses to call the Brainiac REST server (:8600) with shared response types, unit tests, and the server-only test stub.

contexts.name: Publish: Render + Confluence + Git
contexts.description: Publish crate: render knowledge documents and publish them to Confluence (HTTP API), Git, and OKF bundles (Open Knowledge Format, with index/log artifacts and AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md agent pointer blocks).

contexts.name: Console: Pitch / Marketing
contexts.description: Pitch/marketing pages: data-driven diagrams and static narrative content.

contexts.name: Console: Knowledge Base Explainer
contexts.description: Knowledge Base explainer (/kb): illustrated concept page with math-driven SVG diagrams.

contexts.name: Console: Library Explainer
contexts.description: Library explainer (/library): public page for the normative layer (coding standards per stack + agent skills) with deterministic SVG diagrams and honesty-guard tests pinned to docs/LIBRARY-PLAN.md.

instructions: Context map for Brainiac. Each group is a business domain (domain field); each context is a feature with its files (filePaths) + a technical category. Read this to learn which files belong to which feature BEFORE editing. When you change a context's files, update them here (or run Vibeman's refresh) to keep this fresh.
